title: I lint-scanned 36 popular MCP servers. A third of them are failing your agent. · Teng Li
excerpt: Your MCP server can be 100% spec-compliant and still be unusable by an agent. I built mcpgrade to measure the difference — then pointed it at 36 popular servers.
content_html:
2026-07-21AgentsMCPEngineering

Your MCP server can be 100% spec-compliant and still be unusable by an agent.

The Model Context Protocol spec tells you how to transport tools: JSON-RPC framing, capability negotiation, schema shapes. It says nothing about whether a model can actually use what you serve — whether it picks the right tool out of your catalog, fills the arguments correctly, or burns 8k tokens parsing your schemas on every single request.

I integrate first- and third-party MCP connectors into a production AI agent for a living, and I kept seeing the same failure: servers that pass every compliance check, yet the model calls the wrong tool, hallucinates arguments, or ignores the tool entirely. The problems were never in the protocol layer. They were in the parts no one lints: descriptions, naming, schema design.

So I wrote mcpgrade — a Lighthouse-style scorecard for MCP servers. One command, no API key, report in seconds:

npx mcpgrade --stdio "npx -y your-mcp-server"

Then I pointed it at 36 popular servers. It did not go great.

The results

Full sortable table: tengli.dev/mcp-leaderboard.html. The short version (static analysis, point-in-time snapshot; servers marked (archived) are unmaintained reference implementations, included because they're still widely installed and copied):

Top of the class (A): brave-search (archived), exa, google-maps (archived), slack (archived), perplexity-ask, @shopify/dev-mcp, @apify/actors-mcp-server, airbnb, figma-developer-mcp, tavily, gitlab (archived), elastic, shrimp-task-manager, and more — 15 of 36.

Bottom of the class (D/F), 11 of 36 — and it's not hobby projects: MongoDB's official server (66, with 66 errors), Notion's official server (62), Airtable (69, 66 errors), todoist-mcp-server (67, 110 errors), GitHub's archived reference server (67, 44 errors), and firecrawl-mcp at the very bottom (57, 134 errors).

Two more servers (Stripe, Supabase) couldn't be scanned with dummy credentials and were excluded rather than graded.

Finding 1: the ecosystem has an undocumented-parameter epidemic

Almost every D/F server has a descriptions score of zero while its schema, naming, and token scores are fine. One rule dominates: D004 — parameter has no description.

firecrawl: 132 of its 134 errors are undocumented parameters. url, formats, jsonOptions — the model gets a name and a type, nothing else. todoist: 110. MongoDB and Airtable: 66 each.

The root cause is visible in the source of nearly all of them: schemas are generated from zod or OpenAPI definitions, and nobody adds .describe(). The type system knows url: string. The model needs to know which URL, in what format, with what constraints. Your schema generator is quietly stripping the single most important signal your tools have.

If you take one thing from this post: open your server, count the parameters without a description, and fix them. It's the highest-leverage hour you can spend on agent reliability.

Finding 2: it's documentation discipline, not catalog size — but size makes discipline harder

My first pass at this data suggested "small catalogs win": most 95+ scorers have few tools, and the 24–26 tool servers cluster at D/F. Then shrimp-task-manager scored A/96 with 15 tools — carefully documented, tightly named, every description distinct.

So the honest version: well-documented big catalogs are possible; they're just rare. Every tool you add is another description to write, another name that can collide, another schema to keep tight. Discipline doesn't scale by default. (Size still taxes you either way: the full catalog is serialized into every request.)

Finding 3: compliance and usability are different axes

The most-updated servers aren't the most usable ones. The archived Slack reference server — code nobody maintains — scores A/97, because someone once documented every tool and every parameter by hand. Meanwhile several actively-developed commercial servers ship parameters with no descriptions at all.

Agent usability is a writing problem more than an engineering problem. Compliance checkers can't measure it. That's the gap mcpgrade fills.

(One hopeful counterpoint: while writing this, context7 shipped a new version that fixed all its missing parameter descriptions — jumping from C to a perfect static score. The ecosystem can move fast when the gap is visible.)

Finding 4: I checked the static scores against a real model. The scary number is refusal.

Static lint is a proxy, so I built --eval: it synthesizes realistic single-step tasks (each embedding concrete values for every required parameter), shows a model the full catalog, and measures whether it picks the right tool and fills valid arguments. Calibration details and methodology: docs/eval-calibration.md. Cost: pennies per server on a small model.

Two results worth your attention:

Static findings predict live confusion. On well-documented servers, tool-selection accuracy was 100%. On firecrawl it dropped to 84% — and the misses land exactly on the naming collisions static rules flag: extract↔scrape, agent_status↔check_crawl_status, feedback↔search_feedback.

Big fuzzy catalogs break refusal. Given deliberately out-of-scope tasks, the model correctly declined 100% of the time on small, well-documented catalogs — but only 50% of the time on firecrawl's 26 fuzzy tools. Half the time it "found" a plausible tool and called it. In production, that's an agent doing something when it should do nothing — arguably the most dangerous failure mode there is.

What "good" looks like

From the top scorers, a checklist:

  • Every tool description answers three questions: what it does, when to use it, what it returns.
  • Every parameter has a description with format and one example value.
  • Fixed value sets live in enum, not in prose.
  • required is declared explicitly — even when it's empty.
  • One naming convention, verb_object style, no generic verbs, no near-twin names.
  • Errors name the missing/invalid parameter so the model can self-correct in one turn.

Try it on your server

npx mcpgrade --stdio "node ./my-server.js"   # local stdio
npx mcpgrade https://my-server.example/mcp    # streamable HTTP
npx mcpgrade <target> --fail-on error         # CI gate
npx mcpgrade <target> --eval                  # live model test (BYO key; any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works)

24 rules, each with a concrete fix and a rationale you're welcome to dispute in the issues — the ruleset is opinionated by design, and I'd rather have the argument in public. (How this differs from mcp-lint and other MCP QA tools — with side-by-side outputs: docs/comparison.md.)

If you maintain one of the servers above and fix your score, open a rescan issue — I'll happily re-run and update the table. PRs to your own servers beat arguments with my ruleset.

I build production AI agent integrations at a large tech company; mcpgrade is a personal project and reflects scars from integrating dozens of MCP connectors. No affiliation with any server ranked above.

← All writing

sentences.en: The ecosystem can move fast when the gap is visible.)
sentences.zh: 当差距可见时，生态系统可以快速发展。）

blocks.html_en: Finding 4: I checked the static scores against a real model. The scary number is refusal.

sentences.en: Finding 4: I checked the static scores against a real model.
sentences.zh: 发现 4：我对照真实模型检查了静态分数。

sentences.en: The scary number is refusal.
sentences.zh: 可怕的数字是拒绝。

blocks.html_en: Static lint is a proxy, so I built --eval: it synthesizes realistic single-step tasks (each embedding concrete values for every required parameter), shows a model the full catalog, and measures whether it picks the right tool and fills valid arguments. Calibration details and methodology: docs/eval-calibration.md. Cost: pennies per server on a small model.

sentences.en: Static lint is a proxy, so I built --eval: it synthesizes realistic single-step tasks (each embedding concrete values for every required parameter), shows a model the full catalog, and measures whether it picks the right tool and fills valid arguments.
sentences.zh: 静态 lint 是一个代理，所以我构建了 --eval：它综合实际的单步任务（每个任务为每个所需参数嵌入具体值），显示模型的完整目录，并衡量它是否选择了正确的工具并填充了有效的参数。

sentences.en: Calibration details and methodology: docs/eval-calibration.md.
sentences.zh: 校准详细信息和方法：docs/eval-calibration.md。

sentences.en: Cost: pennies per server on a small model.
sentences.zh: 成本：小型型号的每台服务器几美分。

blocks.html_en: Two results worth your attention:

sentences.en: Two results worth your attention:
sentences.zh: 有两个结果值得您关注：

blocks.html_en: Static findings predict live confusion. On well-documented servers, tool-selection accuracy was 100%. On firecrawl it dropped to 84% — and the misses land exactly on the naming collisions static rules flag: extract↔scrape, agent_status↔check_crawl_status, feedback↔search_feedback.

sentences.en: Static findings predict live confusion.
sentences.zh: 静态发现预示着现场混乱。

sentences.en: On well-documented servers, tool-selection accuracy was 100%.
sentences.zh: 在记录良好的服务器上，工具选择的准确性为 100%。

sentences.en: On firecrawl it dropped to 84% — and the misses land exactly on the naming collisions static rules flag: extract↔scrape, agent_status↔check_crawl_status, feedback↔search_feedback.
sentences.zh: 在 firecrawl 上，它下降到 84%，并且未命中的情况正好落在命名冲突静态规则标志上：extract↔scrape、agent_status↔check_crawl_status、feedback↔search_feedback。

blocks.html_en: Big fuzzy catalogs break refusal. Given deliberately out-of-scope tasks, the model correctly declined 100% of the time on small, well-documented catalogs — but only 50% of the time on firecrawl's 26 fuzzy tools. Half the time it "found" a plausible tool and called it. In production, that's an agent doing something when it should do nothing — arguably the most dangerous failure mode there is.

sentences.en: Big fuzzy catalogs break refusal.
sentences.zh: 大的模糊目录打破了拒绝。

sentences.en: Given deliberately out-of-scope tasks, the model correctly declined 100% of the time on small, well-documented catalogs — but only 50% of the time on firecrawl's 26 fuzzy tools.
sentences.zh: 考虑到故意超出范围的任务，该模型在小型、有详细记录的目录上正确拒绝的时间为 100%，但在 firecrawl 的 26 个模糊工具上只有 50% 的正确拒绝时间。

sentences.en: Half the time it "found" a plausible tool and called it.
sentences.zh: 有一半的时间它“找到”了一个看似合理的工具并调用它。

sentences.en: In production, that's an agent doing something when it should do nothing — arguably the most dangerous failure mode there is.
sentences.zh: 在生产中，这是代理在不应该执行任何操作时执行某些操作 - 可以说是最危险的故障模式。

blocks.html_en: What "good" looks like

sentences.en: What "good" looks like
sentences.zh: “好”是什么样的

blocks.html_en: From the top scorers, a checklist:

sentences.en: From the top scorers, a checklist:
sentences.zh: 从得分最高的球员那里得到一份清单：

blocks.html_en: • Every tool description answers three questions: what it does, when to use it, what it returns.

sentences.en: Every tool description answers three questions: what it does, when to use it, what it returns.
sentences.zh: 每个工具描述都回答三个问题：它的作用、何时使用它、它返回什么。

blocks.html_en: • Every parameter has a description with format and one example value.

sentences.en: Every parameter has a description with format and one example value.
sentences.zh: 每个参数都有一个带有格式的描述和一个示例值。

blocks.html_en: • Fixed value sets live in enum, not in prose.

sentences.en: Fixed value sets live in enum, not in prose.
sentences.zh: 固定值集存在于枚举中，而不是散文中。

blocks.html_en: • required is declared explicitly — even when it's empty.

sentences.en: required is declared explicitly — even when it's empty.
sentences.zh: required 是显式声明的——即使它是空的。

blocks.html_en: • One naming convention, verb_object style, no generic verbs, no near-twin names.

sentences.en: One naming convention, verb_object style, no generic verbs, no near-twin names.
sentences.zh: 一种命名约定，verb_object 样式，没有通用动词，没有近乎双胞胎的名称。

blocks.html_en: • Errors name the missing/invalid parameter so the model can self-correct in one turn.

sentences.en: Errors name the missing/invalid parameter so the model can self-correct in one turn.
sentences.zh: 错误会命名丢失/无效的参数，以便模型可以一轮自我纠正。

blocks.html_en: Try it on your server

sentences.en: Try it on your server
sentences.zh: 在你的服务器上尝试一下

blocks.html_en: 24 rules, each with a concrete fix and a rationale you're welcome to dispute in the issues — the ruleset is opinionated by design, and I'd rather have the argument in public. (How this differs from mcp-lint and other MCP QA tools — with side-by-side outputs: docs/comparison.md.)

sentences.en: 24 rules, each with a concrete fix and a rationale you're welcome to dispute in the issues — the ruleset is opinionated by design, and I'd rather have the argument in public.
sentences.zh: 24 条规则，每条规则都有具体的解决方案和理由，欢迎您就问题提出争议——规则集是经过设计的，我宁愿公开争论。

sentences.en: (How this differs from mcp-lint and other MCP QA tools — with side-by-side outputs: docs/comparison.md.)
sentences.zh: （这与 mcp-lint 和其他 MCP QA 工具有何不同 - 具有并行输出：docs/comparison.md。）

blocks.html_en: If you maintain one of the servers above and fix your score, open a rescan issue — I'll happily re-run and update the table. PRs to your own servers beat arguments with my ruleset.

sentences.en: If you maintain one of the servers above and fix your score, open a rescan issue — I'll happily re-run and update the table.
sentences.zh: 如果您维护上述服务器之一并修复您的分数，请打开重新扫描问题 - 我会很乐意重新运行并更新表格。

sentences.en: PRs to your own servers beat arguments with my ruleset.
sentences.zh: 您自己的服务器的 PR 可以用我的规则集击败争论。

blocks.html_en: I build production AI agent integrations at a large tech company; mcpgrade is a personal project and reflects scars from integrating dozens of MCP connectors. No affiliation with any server ranked above.

sentences.en: I build production AI agent integrations at a large tech company; mcpgrade is a personal project and reflects scars from integrating dozens of MCP connectors.
sentences.zh: 我在一家大型科技公司构建生产型人工智能代理集成； mcpgrade 是一个个人项目，反映了集成数十个 MCP 连接器带来的伤痕。

sentences.en: No affiliation with any server ranked above.
sentences.zh: 与排名以上的任何服务器没有任何隶属关系。

blocks.html_en: ← All writing

sentences.en: ← All writing
sentences.zh: ← 所有写作

content_html_zh:
2026-07-21代理MCP工程

您的 MCP 服务器可以 100% 符合规范，但仍然无法被代理使用。

模型上下文协议规范告诉您如何传输工具：JSON-RPC 框架、功能协商、模式形状。 它没有说明模型是否可以真正使用您所提供的服务 - 它是否从您的目录中选择正确的工具，正确填充参数，或者在每个请求上解析您的模式时燃烧 8k 令牌。

我将第一方和第三方 MCP 连接器集成到生产 AI 代理中以谋生，但我不断看到同样的失败：服务器通过了每项合规性检查，但模型调用了错误的工具、产生幻觉或完全忽略该工具。 问题从来不在协议层。 它们位于无人察觉的部分：描述、命名、模式设计。

所以我编写了 mcpgrade——一个用于 MCP 服务器的 Lighthouse 风格的记分卡。 一条命令，无需 API 密钥，几秒内即可报告：

npx mcpgrade --stdio "npx -y your-mcp-server"

然后我把它指向了36个流行的服务器。 进展并不顺利。

结果

完整的可排序表：tengli.dev/mcp-leaderboard.html。 简短版本（静态分析、时间点快照；标记（存档）的服务器是未维护的参考实现，包括在内是因为它们仍然广泛安装和复制）：

一流 (A)：brave-search（已存档）、exa、google-maps（已存档）、slack（已存档）、perplexity-ask、@shopify/dev-mcp、@apify/actors-mcp-server、airbnb、figma-developer-mcp、tavily、gitlab（已存档）、elastic、shrimp-task-manager 等 — 36 个中的 15 个。

班级垫底 (D/F)，36 个中的 11 个 — 这不是业余爱好项目：MongoDB 的官方服务器（66，有 66 个错误）、Notion 的官方服务器（62）、Airtable（69、66 个错误）、todoist-mcp-server（67、110 个错误）、GitHub 的存档参考服务器（67、44 个错误）以及最底部的 firecrawl-mcp （57、134 个错误）。

另外两台服务器（Stripe、Supabase）无法使用虚拟凭据进行扫描，因此被排除而不是评分。

发现 1：生态系统存在未记录参数的流行病

几乎每个 D/F 服务器的描述分数为零，而其架构、命名和令牌分数都很好。 一条规则占主导地位：D004 — 参数没有描述。

firecrawl：其 134 个错误中有 132 个是未记录的参数。 url、formats、jsonOptions — 模型获取名称和类型，仅此而已。 待办事项：110。 MongoDB 和 Airtable：各 66 个。

根本原因在几乎所有这些的源中都是可见的：模式是从 zod 或 OpenAPI 定义生成的，并且没有人添加 .describe()。 类型系统知道 url: string。 模型需要知道哪个 URL、采用什么格式、有什么约束。 您的模式生成器正在悄悄地剥离您的工具所拥有的最重要的信号。

如果您从这篇文章中学到一件事：打开您的服务器，计算没有描述的参数，然后修复它们。 这是您可以在座席可靠性方面花费的最高杠杆时间。

发现 2：这是文档纪律，而不是目录大小 — 但规模使纪律变得更加困难

我第一次查看此数据表明“小目录获胜”：大多数 95+ 评分者几乎没有工具，并且 24-26 个工具服务器集群在 D/F。 然后，虾任务管理器使用 15 个工具获得了 A/96 的分数——仔细记录、严格命名、每个描述都清晰可见。

所以诚实的版本：记录良好的大目录是可能的；他们只是很少见。 您添加的每个工具都是另一个需要编写的描述，另一个可能发生冲突的名称，另一个需要保持紧密的模式。 默认情况下，纪律不会扩展。 （无论哪种方式，大小仍然会给您带来负担：完整的目录会序列化到每个请求中。）

发现 3：合规性和可用性是不同的轴

最新更新的服务器并不是最可用的服务器。 存档的 Slack 参考服务器（无人维护的代码）得分为 A/97，因为有人曾经手动记录了每个工具和每个参数。 与此同时，一些积极开发的商业服务器所提供的参数根本没有任何描述。

代理可用性与其说是一个工程问题，不如说是一个编写问题。 合规检查员无法衡量它。 这就是 mcpgrade 填补的空白。

（一个充满希望的对比：在写这篇文章时，context7 发布了一个新版本，修复了所有缺失的参数描述——从 C 跳到完美的静态分数。 当差距可见时，生态系统可以快速发展。）

发现 4：我对照真实模型检查了静态分数。 可怕的数字是拒绝。

静态 lint 是一个代理，所以我构建了 --eval：它综合实际的单步任务（每个任务为每个所需参数嵌入具体值），显示模型的完整目录，并衡量它是否选择了正确的工具并填充了有效的参数。 校准详细信息和方法：docs/eval-calibration.md。 成本：小型型号的每台服务器几美分。

有两个结果值得您关注：

静态发现预示着现场混乱。 在记录良好的服务器上，工具选择的准确性为 100%。 在 firecrawl 上，它下降到 84%，并且未命中的情况正好落在命名冲突静态规则标志上：extract↔scrape、agent_status↔check_crawl_status、feedback↔search_feedback。

大的模糊目录打破了拒绝。 考虑到故意超出范围的任务，该模型在小型、有详细记录的目录上正确拒绝的时间为 100%，但在 firecrawl 的 26 个模糊工具上只有 50% 的正确拒绝时间。 有一半的时间它“找到”了一个看似合理的工具并调用它。 在生产中，这是代理在不应该执行任何操作时执行某些操作 - 可以说是最危险的故障模式。

“好”是什么样的

从得分最高的球员那里得到一份清单：

  • 每个工具描述都回答三个问题：它的作用、何时使用它、它返回什么。
  • 每个参数都有一个带有格式的描述和一个示例值。
  • 固定值集存在于枚举中，而不是散文中。
  • required 是显式声明的——即使它是空的。
  • 一种命名约定，verb_object 样式，没有通用动词，没有近乎双胞胎的名称。
  • 错误会命名丢失/无效的参数，以便模型可以一轮自我纠正。

在你的服务器上尝试一下

npx mcpgrade --stdio "node ./my-server.js"   # local stdio
npx mcpgrade https://my-server.example/mcp    # streamable HTTP
npx mcpgrade <target> --fail-on error         # CI gate
npx mcpgrade <target> --eval                  # live model test (BYO key; any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works)

24 条规则，每条规则都有具体的解决方案和理由，欢迎您就问题提出争议——规则集是经过设计的，我宁愿公开争论。 （这与 mcp-lint 和其他 MCP QA 工具有何不同 - 具有并行输出：docs/comparison.md。）

如果您维护上述服务器之一并修复您的分数，请打开重新扫描问题 - 我会很乐意重新运行并更新表格。 您自己的服务器的 PR 可以用我的规则集击败争论。

我在一家大型科技公司构建生产型人工智能代理集成； mcpgrade 是一个个人项目，反映了集成数十个 MCP 连接器带来的伤痕。 与排名以上的任何服务器没有任何隶属关系。
